new_classes.label_en: Wine with PDO in containers ≤ 2 L with actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit g.U. in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L mit einem vorhandenen Alkoholgehalt > 15% vol
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, with an actual alcoholic strength by volume exceeding 15% vol, as classified under CN subheadings 22042111 to 22042178.

coverage_explanation: The three primary discriminating criteria for CN 22042174 are captured: (1) PDO quality via PDOOriginQuality restriction, (2) container volume ≤ 2 L via SmallContainer parthood restriction, and (3) actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol via a decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent. The legal text also references Chapter 22 Additional Note 6(a), which defines the scope of PDO wines and may include further sub-criteria (e.g. specific colour/type categories such as red, white, rosé within the 22042111–22042178 range). The specific sub-classification by colour or grape variety type (which distinguishes 22042174 from its siblings 22042111–22042178) is not captured here because no colour/type quality class exists in the current TBox and the legal note text provided does not enumerate those sub-criteria explicitly. Coverage is therefore estimated at 0.75.
